response.justification: Wei Chen has a strong multi-decade software engineering background and genuine recent contributions to MCP infrastructure and agentic workflows at re:cinq, which directly maps to one of the role's key technology requirements. However, the core qualification gap is disqualifying at staff level: the role demands 8+ years of production-grade AI solution delivery, whereas Chen's AI/GenAI work appears limited to roughly one year. Python—listed as a primary required language—is entirely absent from the resume's skills and project history. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks, and the customer-facing forward-deployment and technical discovery leadership central to this role are not demonstrated.
strengths: Hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) framework and code-generator experience at re:cinq directly aligns with a listed key technology in the job description
Consistent staff-level engineering titles and long-tenured ownership of complex, production systems demonstrate genuine seniority
AWS CDK and TypeScript usage in production at Optilyz provides relevant cloud and language coverage for part of the required stack
concerns: Python—a primary required language—does not appear anywhere in the resume's skills, employment history, or project list
AI/GenAI experience spans roughly one year (April 2025–present); the role requires a minimum of 8 years delivering production AI solutions
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM performance evaluation, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI
